blurb: This is where we go for special occasions when we want to impress out-of-town visitors or celebrate anniversaries. It sits right on the Chattahoochee with big windows overlooking the water, so try to get a table by the river if you can. The atmosphere feels fancy but not stuffy. The seafood is what they're known for, and it shows. My husband always gets their crab cakes and I usually go for whatever fish special they're running. It's definitely a splurge, but the kind of place that makes you feel like you're having a real night out without driving downtown.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is the real deal for French pastries and breakfast, run by people who actually know what they're doing. The bright, cheerful space makes it perfect for weekend brunch or grabbing fresh baguettes for dinner at home. If you want to impress houseguests or treat yourself to something special, this is where you come.

The quiches are excellent for lunch, and the petits fours are almost too pretty to eat. Their baguettes are what you'd expect from a proper French bakery, crusty outside and soft inside. The croissants sell out early on weekends, so don't wait until noon if that's what you're after. Great for a fancy breakfast date, picking up dessert for a dinner party, or when you want to feel a little sophisticated with your morning coffee.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is my go-to when I want Middle Eastern food that's fresh, cheap, and actually good. It's tucked into a shopping center off Franklin Gateway, and the casual setup means you can grab a quick lunch or bring the whole family without any fuss. Great option when you want something healthier than the usual takeout suspects.

The shawarma is what most people come for, and they pile it high with meat and all the fixings. The falafel is crispy and not dry like some places, and they have plenty of vegetarian options if that's your thing. Everything comes with generous portions of hummus and pita. Perfect for a casual dinner when you want something different, or grabbing lunch that won't make you feel gross afterward.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb: I was skeptical about the hype around this place, but it really is that good. The pizza is proper Neapolitan style with a thin crust that somehow manages to be both crispy and chewy. It's in the shopping center on Blue Stone Road, and there's usually a wait because word has gotten out. They use ingredients imported from Italy, which sounds fancy but you can actually taste the difference. The Margherita is perfect if you want to see what all the fuss is about. Great for date night or when you want pizza that's a step up from the usual chains.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is the answer when your family can't agree on where to eat. It's a food hall with twenty different vendors, so everyone can get what they want and still sit together. The setup works great for lunch meetings, family dinners, or when you're exploring the square and want options. Way better than arguing about restaurants in the car.

Walk around and see what looks good because the variety is impressive. Some vendors focus on local favorites while others bring international flavors you won't find elsewhere in Marietta. The retail shops mixed in make it easy to browse after you eat. Perfect for tourists visiting the square, locals who want to try something new, or families with picky eaters who need multiple backup plans.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb: This is the taco place that everyone talks about, and they're not wrong. It's in Sandy Springs Place with a fun, energetic vibe that works for everything from quick lunch to girls' night out. The atmosphere is vibrant and the service is actually helpful, which matters when you're trying to navigate all the options. Start with the Esquite if you like Mexican street corn. It's packed with flavor and sets the tone for the meal. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and perfect for sharing at the table. They're open until midnight most nights, so it's great when you want good tacos late.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a fun place to try it without driving downtown. They bring different cuts of meat right to your table on skewers, and you just signal when you want more or when you need a break. It's perfect for a birthday dinner or when you want to make a meal into an event. The atmosphere is relaxed, not stuffy like some steakhouses.

The cheese bread alone is worth going for, and the feijoada stew gives you a taste of traditional Brazilian comfort food. Don't skip the salad bar either because it's actually good and helps balance out all that meat. Fair warning though, pace yourself because they keep bringing food until you tell them to stop. Great for date nights or celebrating something special when you want to try something different.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is the place you go when someone in your group is craving pancakes at 2am or you need a reliable meal any hour of the day. It's that classic neon-sign diner that's been feeding Marietta for decades, and being open 24/7 makes it a lifesaver for shift workers and late-night cravings. The menu is massive, so don't stress about finding something everyone will eat.

The Greek specialties are what set it apart from your average diner food. Try the gyro or Greek omelet if you want something different, but honestly their regular breakfast is solid too. The portions are generous and the coffee keeps coming. Perfect for Sunday brunch with the family, post-game meals, or when you just need comfort food that won't break the bank.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb: This is the place my family goes when we're craving proper Indian comfort food. It's in a strip mall in Perimeter Center, but don't let that fool you. The inside is colorful and lively, perfect for sharing big family-style platters with a group. The name says it all. The biryani here is what you come for, and they do it right with fragrant rice and tender meat that actually tastes like it took time to make. We always end up ordering way too much food because everything looks good, but that just means great leftovers the next day.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb: My friend dragged me here for girls' night and now I can't stop thinking about going back. It's Persian food done right, with a warm atmosphere that makes you want to linger over dinner. This is definitely date night or special occasion material. The hummus is a good place to start, and the chicken shish kabobs with rice, lentils and raisins are what I dream about. The cocktails are solid too, which isn't always a given at Persian restaurants. Fair warning, they close between lunch and dinner most days, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. But when they're open, it's worth the trip.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
